operations.whyNeeded: Required for safe welding, panel replacement, and to prevent electrical damage during repair. OEM procedures require battery disconnect before structural work.
Verifies battery health after disconnect/reconnect cycle. Batteries can lose charge or develop issues during extended repair times.
Resets electronic modules, clocks, radio presets, seat/mirror memory, and adaptive systems after battery disconnect.
Prevents moisture, debris, and contamination of exposed electrical connectors during repair. Protects wiring harnesses from paint overspray and grinding dust.
Maintains battery charge during extended repair with a battery maintainer/tender to prevent discharge and module memory loss.

operations.description: Charge and Maintain Battery during ADAS
operations.whyNeeded: ADAS calibration procedures require stable voltage. Battery must be fully charged and maintained during the calibration process to prevent false failures.
Safety check to confirm no high voltage present before beginning work on hybrid/EV vehicles or near high-voltage components.
Documents pre-existing DTCs and system status before any repairs begin. Required by most OEM repair procedures and insurer guidelines.
Verifies systems during the repair process, especially after major component replacement or structural work. Catches issues before reassembly.
Confirms all DTCs are cleared and systems functional after repair completion. Required documentation for safe return to customer.
Time to connect, configure, and set up the diagnostic scan tool for vehicle-specific communication protocols.
Road test and verification of dynamic systems including ADAS, steering, braking, and suspension performance after repair.
Research OEM repair procedures, position statements, and technical service bulletins specific to the vehicle being repaired.
Printed diagnostic report documenting ADAS system status, calibration results, and any related DTCs.

operations.description: Simulate Full Fluids for ADAS Calibrations
operations.whyNeeded: ADAS calibration requires vehicle to be at proper ride height. Simulating full fluids (fuel, washer, coolant) ensures correct sensor alignment.

operations.description: Check and Adjust Tire Pressure for ADAS Calibrations
operations.whyNeeded: Proper tire pressure is critical for accurate ADAS calibration. Incorrect pressure affects ride height and sensor angles.

operations.description: Remove Customer Belongings for ADAS Calibrations
operations.whyNeeded: Customer belongings add weight that affects ride height. Must be removed for accurate ADAS calibration to factory specifications.
Time to position targets, fixtures, and calibration equipment per OEM specifications for static/dynamic ADAS calibration.
Final cleaning of vehicle interior and exterior before delivery. Removes repair dust, masking residue, and fingerprints.
Glass cleaner for final detail cleaning of all windows and mirrors after repair and paint work.
Masking materials and labor to protect removed components from damage during storage. Prevents scratches, paint contamination, and hardware loss.
Proper disposal of damaged parts removed during repair. Includes handling, transport, and disposal fees.
Disposal of hazardous materials including paint waste, solvents, and contaminated materials per EPA regulations.
Replacement clips, fasteners, bolts, and hardware that are damaged or single-use during R&I operations.

operations.description: Steering Wheel Cover, Seat Cover, and Floor Mat
operations.whyNeeded: Protective covers installed before repair to prevent interior contamination. Shows professionalism and protects customer's vehicle.
Touch up paint on exposed bolt heads to prevent corrosion and maintain factory appearance after panel replacement or repair.
Steering wheel lock device to secure vehicle during transport and storage in the shop.
Printed refinish materials invoice documenting paint materials used during the repair.
Fire-resistant blankets to protect surrounding areas from sparks, heat, and spatter during welding operations.
Time to set up, calibrate, and test welding equipment including wire feed, gas flow, and amperage settings for the specific material.
Wire, gas, tips, nozzles, and other consumable materials used during welding operations.
Zinc-rich primer applied to weld flanges before welding to maintain corrosion protection at the weld joint.
Test weld on coupon material to verify weld settings and quality before welding on the vehicle. Required by many OEM procedures.

operations.description: Remove and Cleanup Debris from Welding
operations.whyNeeded: Cleanup of weld spatter, grinding dust, and debris from welding operations. Prevents contamination of paint work and interior.
Remove and reinstall electronic components, wiring, and modules within the weld zone to prevent heat damage during welding.
Time to set up measuring equipment (electronic or mechanical) on the vehicle for structural measurement and verification.
Document structural dimensions before and after repair to verify the vehicle is within factory specifications. Required for structural repairs.
Replacement refrigerant and compressor oil after AC system is opened during repair. R1234yf is the current standard refrigerant.
Protect exposed AC lines and fittings from contamination and damage during repair work.
Verify proper operation of heating, AC, and climate control system after repairs that may have affected these components.
Run vehicle to operating temperature to verify cooling system, thermostat, and climate control operation after repairs.
Replacement coolant after cooling system is opened or drained during repair.
Torque all wheel lug nuts to manufacturer specification after wheel R&I. Critical safety item.
Mount tires on wheels and balance for proper operation after tire or wheel replacement.
Replacement wheel balance weights after tire mounting and balancing.
Lubricant paste used during tire mounting to prevent bead damage and ensure proper seating.
Disposal fee for damaged tires that cannot be reused.
Disable supplemental restraint system before working near airbag components to prevent accidental deployment. Re-enable after repairs.
Inspect all SRS components including airbags, sensors, wiring, and connectors for damage or displacement after collision.
Inspect seatbelts for proper operation and retraction, and steering column for damage or misalignment after collision.
Clean up airbag deployment residue (powder, fabric, chemicals) from vehicle interior. Residue can be irritating and must be properly cleaned.
Program or reset the restraint control module after airbag deployment or replacement. Required for proper SRS operation.
Disassemble and dispose of shipping crate for large replacement panels (bed sides, box sides, etc.).
Drain fuel tank for safe body-on-frame separation or fuel system repairs. Replace fuel after reassembly.
Safely depressurize the fuel system before disconnecting fuel lines or removing the fuel tank.
Administrative processing fee for total loss handling, documentation, and coordination.
Vehicle storage fee while awaiting total loss disposition and pickup.
Thorough inspection of recovered stolen vehicle for damage, missing components, and safety concerns.
Clean up fingerprint dusting powder left by law enforcement during investigation of the stolen vehicle.
Replacement wheel lock set to secure wheels after stolen recovery. Original locks may be missing or damaged.
